blocks.title: Broker & cluster
paragraphs: Kafka runs as a distributed cluster of brokers. Each broker stores partition replicas and serves produce/fetch requests. ZooKeeper or KRaft coordinates cluster metadata depending on deployment mode.

items: Producer serializes a record (key, value, headers) and picks a partition (key hash or explicit).
Broker appends the batch to the partition log and acknowledges based on acks config.
Consumer polls, receives batches, processes, then commits offsets (auto or manual).
Retention policies or compaction govern how long data remains addressable by offset.

blocks.title: Delivery semantics (at a glance)
blocks.bodyHtml: At-most-once skips retry on failure; at-least-once retries but may duplicate; exactly-once needs idempotent producers, transactions, and careful consumer offset management.
